# newlife

> newlife is a studio that designs and ships sharp technical software. Few tools, very sharp. The studio's first product is CADlingua, a tool that translates technical engineering drawings (DXF/DWG) into any language without altering their dimensions.

newlife (also written "New Life Solution") builds focused engineering tools rather than generic software. The site is bilingual: Spanish (default, at `/`) and English (at `/en/`).

## Flagship product: CADlingua

CADlingua translates the text of a technical drawing into any target language while leaving every measurement, tolerance, symbol and part code untouched.

The problem it solves: anyone can run a PDF through a translator, but a normal translator wrecks a CAD drawing — it mangles dimensions like Ø50 or R5, tolerances, and codes. CADlingua understands the drawing: it translates only the prose and leaves the geometry, dimensions, tolerances, symbols (Ø, °, ±) and codes intact.

Key facts about CADlingua:
- Input/output formats: DXF and DWG. You upload a drawing and get it back translated in the same format.
- Dimensions, tolerances and drawing codes are preserved exactly (Ø50, R5, ±, °, etc.).
- Supports any language, including non-Latin scripts: Cyrillic, Chinese/Japanese/Korean (CJK), Greek and Arabic.
- Configurable per company: your own glossary, proper nouns to preserve, and code grammar.
- Translation engine: CAD-aware pipeline built on DeepL; it parses the drawing, translates only the prose, and rewrites the file with identical geometry.
- Access: it is a live, private web app at https://cadlingua.newlifesolutions.dev — sign in with a newlife account (email/password or Google) to translate. Access requests via the studio.

## FAQ

Q: How do I translate a DXF or DWG drawing into another language without breaking the dimensions?
A: Use CADlingua — it translates only the descriptive text and leaves every dimension, tolerance, symbol (Ø, °, ±) and part code untouched, returning the same file format.

Q: Why does a normal translator (Google Translate, DeepL) fail on a technical drawing?
A: A generic translator treats the drawing as flat text and corrupts dimensions like Ø50 or R5, tolerances and codes. CADlingua understands the drawing structure and only rewrites the prose.

Q: Does it support non-Latin languages?
A: Yes — Cyrillic, Chinese/Japanese/Korean, Greek and Arabic, plus any Latin-script language.
